title: Bihar: Earphone Explosion Leaves Man Injured Amid Extreme Heat in Chapra - lokmattimes.com
full_text:
Bihar has witnessed a severe heatwave and due to this scorching heat, the internal temperatures of electronic devices have risen to such an extent that they have actually started to explode. Amidst this situation, a shocking incident has emerged from the Chapra district. The impact of the continuously rising temperatures in the Saran region is now becoming apparent in electronic gadgets as well.
In Chapra, a young man, Ghanshyam Mishra, suffered severe burns when his Bluetooth earphone exploded while he was riding his bike. Local residents quickly responded, preventing a major tragedy. Mishra was immediately taken to Chapra Sadar Hospital for treatment. He reported that the explosion occurred four kilometers outside Chapra as he was returning from the Lahladpur market, and he felt intense heat before the device detonated.
Terrified by the incident, Ghanshyam Mishra immediately brought his bike to a halt. With the assistance of people present nearby, he managed to remove his burning clothes. He sustained burns in the accident. Local residents promptly admitted him to the Chapra Sadar Hospital, where doctors administered treatment. This incident has heightened concerns regarding the usage of electronic devices during periods of extreme heat.
Electrical Engineer Kailash Kumar stated that battery-operated, rechargeable devices are the most vulnerable to the adverse effects of extreme temperatures. Such explosions can occur due to factors such as a continuous Bluetooth connection, the surrounding hot environment, and battery overheating. They have advised citizens that mobile phones, Bluetooth earphones, power banks, and other electronic devices should be used with extreme caution during the summer days.
